metadata.description: Avocado crop calendar for Kenya, derived from COLEAD, BIF-CDAAC, Griesbach/ICRAF, and KALRO literature
sources: BIF Avocado Production Manual - CDAAC Kenya (2019)
Avocado Growing in Kenya - Jürgen Griesbach, ICRAF (2005)
Persea Mite Management in Avocados - Samuel Khabi, KALRO (2025)
notes: Calendar is for Kenya's central/eastern highlands (800-2100m), the primary commercial zone
Bimodal rainfall: Long rains Mar-May, Short rains Oct-Dec
Timing shifts by ±2-4 weeks depending on altitude, microclimate, and year
Fuerte and Hass are the two dominant commercial/export varieties

irrigation.action: Begin irrigation — mid-January to April is the most critical water period. Apply 40-50 m³/ha/day via drip.

fertilizer.action: Leaf sampling for nutrient analysis — collect from stress-free trees, 2 months before expected flowering.

pruning.action: Not recommended this month — too close to flowering window.

spraying.action: Low disease pressure in dry conditions. Monitor only.

pest_monitoring.action: Persea mite risk rising (dry season). Scout every 7-10 days. FCM risk moderate in warm areas.

planting.action: Prepare planting holes during dry season. Fill with manure + DSP at least 3 weeks before transplanting.

irrigation.action: Continue irrigation. This is the driest period. Monitor for leaf wilting and fruit shrinking — emergency indicators.

fertilizer.action: Apply boron foliar spray if levels <40 ppm — flowering is approaching (Aug-Oct). Apply zinc only during new leaf flush.

spraying.action: Low disease pressure. Prepare inputs for rainy season.

pest_monitoring.action: Persea mite at peak dry-season risk. Thrips may increase. Set up fruit fly traps if not already done.

planting.action: Finish planting hole preparation. Dig holes, fill with soil/manure/DSP mix.

irrigation.action: Rains starting — reduce or stop irrigation. Ensure drainage is functional.

fertilizer.action: Apply CAN when soil is moist from rains (but not waterlogged). Split nitrogen into 2-3 applications.

pruning.action: Not recommended during wet season — wound infection risk.

spraying.action: Apply mefenoxam soil drench (Ridomil) for Phytophthora prevention at rain onset. Apply copper fungicide for anthracnose at first dry window.

pest_monitoring.action: Fruit fly traps must be active — warm + wet conditions favour breeding. Monitor for FCM. Persea mite pressure declining (rain suppression).

planting.action: OPTIMAL PLANTING WINDOW — long rains onset. Transplant seedlings once soil is deeply wetted.

harvest.action: FUERTE HARVEST BEGINS. Conduct maturity test (7-10 days at 27°C). Harvest only on dry mornings. Pre-cool to 5-7°C within 6 hours.

irrigation.action: No irrigation needed — peak rainfall. Focus on drainage.

fertilizer.action: Second CAN application if split dosing. Apply to moist soil, not before heavy rain (leaching risk).

spraying.action: Copper fungicide every 14 days during wet weather for anthracnose + cercospora. Second phosphonate application 16-20 weeks after first.

pest_monitoring.action: Fruit fly peak — destroy all fallen fruit. FCM monitoring continues. Possible off-season flowering (Mar-Apr) — avoid insecticides near flowers.

harvest.action: FUERTE HARVEST CONTINUES. Pick only on dry days. Wet-harvested fruit has higher rot incidence.

fertilizer.action: Third CAN application if split. Avoid excess nitrogen before flowering.

pruning.action: Not yet — wait for dry conditions post-harvest.

spraying.action: Continue copper fungicide program every 14 days if wet weather persists.

pest_monitoring.action: Fruit fly remains high risk. Weed at end of rainy season to reduce pest/disease reservoirs.

harvest.action: Fuerte harvest ending. Early Pinkerton harvest may begin.

irrigation.action: Soil moisture from long rains still present. Monitor; begin irrigation if wilting observed.

fertilizer.action: Reduce fertilizer rates in dry conditions without irrigation.

pruning.action: OPTIMAL PRUNING WINDOW — Fuerte post-harvest, before flowering (Aug-Sep). Remove dead wood, shape canopy, max height 5.5-7m. Sterilise tools between trees. Prune in dry conditions only.

spraying.action: Disease pressure declining with dry weather. Reduce fungicide program.

pest_monitoring.action: Persea mite rebound alert — transition from wet to dry. Scout every 5-7 days. Thrips risk rising. FCM risk rising in warm conditions.

harvest.action: HASS HARVEST BEGINS. Maturity test: fruit softens without shrivelling at 27°C in 1 week. Pick morning, pre-cool immediately.

irrigation.action: Generally not needed. Monitor soil moisture.

fertilizer.action: Leaf sampling 2 months before flowering — collect now for Aug-Sep flowering analysis.

pruning.action: Continue pruning window for Fuerte. Dormant season is ideal.

spraying.action: Low disease pressure. Focus on post-harvest fruit treatments.

pest_monitoring.action: Persea mite at high dry-season risk. Thrips at peak in hot dry weather. FCM active — maintain pheromone traps.

harvest.action: HASS PEAK HARVEST. Prioritise dry mornings. Cold chain critical in warm weather — pre-cool within 2 hours if ambient >26°C.

irrigation.action: Irrigation needed Aug-Sep for flowering support. Light, frequent applications ≤50mm. Critical for Fuerte flowering + Hass fruit fill.

fertilizer.action: Apply boron foliar spray before flowering if levels <40 ppm. Apply nitrogen after irreversible commitment to flowering to reduce alternate bearing.

pruning.action: Stop pruning — flowering is starting. Any pruning now removes flower buds.

spraying.action: NO INSECTICIDES during flowering — kills bees. Fungicides OK if bee-safe. Prepare copper program for upcoming rains.

pest_monitoring.action: Bee hives in place (2-5/ha). Monitor pollinators. Persea mite still high risk. FCM active.

harvest.action: Hass harvest continues. Late-season fruit ripens faster — move quickly to packhouse.

irrigation.action: Critical irrigation month — flowering needs moisture. Light showers during flowering do NOT harm flowers.

fertilizer.action: Post-anthesis nitrogen to reduce alternate bearing. Apply to moist soil.

spraying.action: NO insecticides near flowers. Bee-safe fungicides only if needed. Begin copper program at end of flowering.

pest_monitoring.action: Protect pollinators. Thrips in flowers — scout, if >5 per cluster apply spinosad in evening after bees retire. Persea mite remains high.

harvest.action: Hass harvest ending. Late varieties (Nabal, Reed) may still be picked.

irrigation.action: Short rains starting — reduce irrigation as soil wets. Ensure the 2-month post-fruit-set window for Fuerte has adequate moisture.

fertilizer.action: Apply CAN to moist soil. Zinc foliar during new flush.

pruning.action: Not recommended — Hass is flowering, Fuerte has young fruit.

spraying.action: Apply mefenoxam soil drench at rain onset (Phytophthora). Begin copper fungicide program for anthracnose — spray at first dry window. Apply azoxystrobin at pigeon-egg fruit size for cercospora.

pest_monitoring.action: Persea mite declining with rains. Fruit fly traps essential — Hass fruit set beginning. Set FCM pheromone traps.

planting.action: SECOND PLANTING WINDOW — short rains onset. Transplant seedlings.

irrigation.action: No irrigation needed — short rains active. Ensure drainage.

fertilizer.action: Second CAN application. Post-fruit-set period for Hass — ensure adequate nutrition.

spraying.action: Continue copper fungicide every 14 days during wet weather. Second phosphonate application if first was in March (16-20 weeks later).

pest_monitoring.action: Persea mite suppressed by rain. Monitor fruit fly. FCM monitoring. Check young Hass fruit for thrips damage (critical 2-3 weeks after fruit set).

irrigation.action: Rains ending. Begin monitoring soil moisture for January dry period.

fertilizer.action: No major application. Prepare inputs for January.

pruning.action: Light pruning possible as rains end, if dry window available.

spraying.action: Last fungicide application of wet season if conditions warrant.

pest_monitoring.action: Persea mite rebound alert — transition from wet to dry. Begin scouting every 5-7 days. FCM eggs sensitive to cold.

dormancy.description: Tree at rest, minimal growth. Triggered by 2-month dry period and/or cool temperatures + short days.

flowering.description: Inflorescences open. Type A: female morning Day 1, male afternoon Day 2. Type B: reversed. Bees are primary pollinators.

fruit_set.description: Pollinated flowers develop into small fruits. Two natural drop periods: pea-size and egg-size. Most critical water period.

fruit_enlargement.description: Fruit grows to full size. Susceptible to cercospora (quarter to three-quarter of full size), anthracnose infection (dormant until ripening), and FCM boring.
fruit_enlargement.note: Duration varies enormously by race: West Indian 5-8mo, Mexican 6-8mo, Guatemalan 10-15mo

harvest.description: Fruit at physiological maturity. Maturity test: fruit softens without shrivelling at 27°C in 7-10 days. Harvest only in dry conditions, morning preferred.

post_harvest.description: Orchard recovery period. Pruning, sanitation, input preparation for next cycle.

fungicide_program.phosphonate_timing: First at rain onset, second 16-20 weeks later
fungicide_program.copper_sprays_per_season: Minimum 3 full cover at 4-week intervals; every 14 days in extended wet weather

Hass_A.note: In Kenya, extensive overlapping within one flower type observed, so self-pollination occurs but cross-pollination improves yield
